summary: Completed comprehensive testing of P1 (Offline Mode/Service Worker) and P2 (WebSocket Chat) features. All 15 backend tests passed (5 REST chat, 8 WebSocket, 2 service worker endpoints). Frontend UI tests verified Chat page with WebSocket status indicator, online count, message sending, and moderation controls.
passed_tests: Backend: GET /api/chat returns chat messages list
Backend: POST /api/chat creates new message
Backend: PUT /api/chat/{id}/hide hides/unhides message (admin)
Backend: DELETE /api/chat/{id} deletes message (admin)
Backend: Chat endpoints require authentication
Backend: WebSocket connects with valid JWT token
Backend: WebSocket rejects connection without token (code 4001)
Backend: WebSocket rejects invalid token (code 4003)
Backend: WebSocket sends and receives messages
Backend: WebSocket ping/pong heartbeat works
Backend: WebSocket typing indicator broadcasts
Backend: WebSocket delete message broadcasts
Backend: WebSocket hide message broadcasts
Backend: All cacheable API endpoints accessible (courses, auth/me, my-progress, groups/my, chat, messages/inbox, enrollments/my)
Backend: Service worker file (sw.js) served correctly
Frontend: Login works with admin credentials
Frontend: Chat page loads with 'Community Chat' header
Frontend: WebSocket status indicator shows 'Live' when connected
Frontend: Online count displays (showed '1' user)
Frontend: Chat input field and send button present
Frontend: Message sending works (via WebSocket)
Frontend: Chat messages display with avatars and timestamps
Frontend: Moderation controls (hide/delete) visible for admin
Frontend: OfflineBanner component integrated (hidden when online)
Frontend: Typing indicator element exists (hidden when no one typing)
critical_code_review_comments: WebSocket implementation is solid with proper JWT auth via query param
ConnectionManager correctly handles room-based messaging (group_id or __global__)
useWebSocket hook has proper reconnection logic with exponential backoff
Service worker v2 correctly caches API responses for offline mode
OfflineBanner uses navigator.onLine for offline detection
Chat.jsx properly falls back to REST polling when WebSocket disconnected
Minor: Console shows 'WebSocket is closed before connection established' on page navigation - this is expected behavior during cleanup
context_for_next_testing_agent: P1 and P2 features fully tested and working. WebSocket chat at /api/ws/chat with JWT auth via query param. REST fallback endpoints at /api/chat. Service worker at /sw.js caches API responses for offline mode. OfflineBanner shows amber banner when navigator.onLine is false. Chat page shows 'Live' green badge when WebSocket connected, 'Polling' amber badge when disconnected. Online count displays when WebSocket connected.

p2_websocket_chat.online_count: Sent on connect and user_left events
p2_websocket_chat.status_indicator: Live (green) when connected, Polling (amber) when disconnected

p1_service_worker.strategy: Network-first for API, Cache-first for static

p1_offline_banner.behavior: Shows amber banner 'You're offline' when navigator.onLine is false
